column1: # author_crosswalk.csv  --  EDIT ME. Hand-curated byline canonicalisation for the
# Each row maps a RAW byline (after normalisation: lower-cased
# CANONICAL author name (or an organisation bucket).
#   match_type : exact   -> normalised byline must equal `pattern` exactly
#                            normalised byline (use for families of ad strings)
#                            article TITLE; matching Unknown/blank-byline articles are
#                            assigned `canonical`. Best for serialized columns with a
#                signature-> `pattern` is a PEN NAME (whitespace/hyphen-flexible) appended to
#                            the article TITLE (e.g. "A Prince of Ur - Homespun") of an
#                            Unknown/blank article. TITLE-ONLY: body text is not scanned
#                            because common-word pen names (homespun) match unrelated prose.
#   pattern    : the normalised string (exact) or a regex (regex). LOWER CASE.
#   canonical  : the display name to assign. For orgs use a bucket label.
#                - person    : a real full-name byline / clear variant  (HIGH confidence)
#                - pseudonym : a documented pen name -> real person      (MED confidence)
#                - initials  : an initials-only byline inferred to a person by a
#                - org       : advertisement / masthead / society filler; NOT an author
#   note       : free text (why); ignored by the code.
# HOW TO EXTEND: add rows. `exact`/`pseudonym`/`initials` rows win over the built-in
# ORG regex; `regex kind=org` rows add to it. Re-run scripts\author_attribution.py {K}.
# Only seed mappings you can defend -- initials rows are explicit inferences.
# ---- Woman's Exponent : Emmeline B. Wells (editor; multiple pen names) ----
